Petition To Establish E'Mayah Johnson Settlement Protection Trust; To Set Bond, To Transfer Venue; And To Fix And Allow Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-12-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

The Court expressed concern about the provision that opens up the trust for termination when the Minor turns 18, the provision that gives the Trustee unfettered authority to determine heirs at law at the time of the Minor's death, the ambiguity of the use of the word "her" in section 5.03 (who is it referring to), etc. Mr. Urbatsch stated that the trust is being established under probate code 3611(g), that it requires that the trust be revocable at age 18, that the Parent is worried that when a Minor receives a check for a substantial amount of money it may not be spent well or protected, that there are concerns about maturity so it gives the Minor the option to not revoke the trust, and it remains in trust until the age of 30 and the Trustee continues to manage it. Mr. Urbatsch also stated that there's not unfettered authority with respect to the Trustee determining heirs, that the code section states who the heirs will be, that it's just figuring out who the people would be to fill that role and that they must comply with California Law as to who the heirs would be. Mr. Urbatsch stated that he can either delete language regarding the heirs or add a phrase, and that he can specify who "her" is. The Court raised the issue of the amount of funds being used to establish the trust being less than the amount that was awarded in the Civil matter, and questions why that is. Mr. Urbatsch stated that he had discussion with the trial Attorney regarding the amount of the settlement and the County, but that he did not participate in the compromise. The Court granted the Petition to Establish The E'Mayah Johnson Settlement Trust; to Set Bond; to Transfer Venue; and to Fix and Allow Attorney Fees and Costs (subject to receiving a revised order that reflects the changes as discussed and review of the order). Status on filing of bond is set for October 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on change of venue is also set for October 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
